etymology.description: 冂 (city outskirts) + 入 (to enter) -> entering the city through its outskirts -> inside, within
etymology.interpretation: The character represents the concept of entering (入) through the outskirts (冂) of a city, metaphorically conveying the sense of being inside or within a boundary.
etymology.simplification: 内: 入(入) (rù, "to enter") + 冂(冂) (jiōng, "house") was simplified while maintaining the semantic components, shifting focus to 'bringing into the house'.

examples.chinese: 房间里面(房間裡面)有一盏灯(有一盞燈)。
examples.pinyin: Fángjiān lǐmiàn yǒu yī zhǎn dēng.
examples.english: Inside the room, there is a lamp.

examples.chinese: 请在车内等候(請在車內等候)。
冰箱内(冰箱內)的东西(的東西)需要保鲜(需要保鮮)。
examples.pinyin: Bīngxiāng nèi de dōngxi xūyào bǎoxiān.
examples.english: The things inside the refrigerator need to be kept fresh.
